content.description:
Tags in Hobson are a universal classification system. Any item can be tagged. Tags themselves are items, enabling metadata, hierarchy, and rich queries.

## Core Concept: Any Item as Tag

There is no dedicated "tag type." Any item can serve as a tag — a note, a project, a concept. An item is "tagged with" another item by including its ID in `content.tags`:

```javascript
{
  id: "my-note-uuid",
  type: "note-type-uuid",
  content: {
    description: "Some note content",
    tags: ["project-uuid", "urgent-tag-uuid", "concept-uuid"]
  }
}
```

This means tags are just items you reference. No special infrastructure needed.

## Tag Discovery Algorithm

To find items tagged with a given tag, query all items whose `content.tags` array includes the tag's ID:

```javascript
const tagged = await api.query(item =>
  item.content?.tags?.includes(tagId)
);
```

## Hierarchical Tags via `content.parent`

Tags can form hierarchies through the `content.parent` property:

```
Hobson (parent: null)
├── Hobson/kernel (parent: hobson-tag-id)
├── Hobson/views (parent: hobson-tag-id)
└── Hobson/idea (parent: hobson-tag-id)
```

Tag queries can walk parent chains to find all items under a category:

```javascript
async function isDescendantOf(tagId, ancestorId) {
  let current = tagId;
  const visited = new Set();
  while (current && !visited.has(current)) {
    if (current === ancestorId) return true;
    visited.add(current);
    const tag = await api.get(current);
    current = tag?.content?.parent;
  }
  return false;
}
```

## Tag Browser

Views can display items organized by their tags. The spatial canvas view supports a tag sidebar that shows hierarchical tags and filters the displayed items.

## Tag Editing UI

The `field-view-tags` field view provides tag editing: search existing tags, create new ones, remove tags. It's used by `system:generic-view` when rendering the `tags` field.

## Design Rationale

### Why Not a Dedicated Tag Type?

A dedicated tag type would be simpler but limiting:
- Any item can already serve as a classification marker
- Projects, people, concepts — all natural tags without a special type
- Tag metadata (color, description) is just item content
- Tag hierarchy is just `content.parent` — works on any item

### Why Not Relationship Items?

"Note A is tagged with Work" — the "is tagged with" doesn't add semantic meaning. It's classification, not a relationship. Compare with "Note A follows on from Note B" where the relationship type carries important information.

Therefore: tags as references in an array, relationships as first-class items.

### Why Universal (Not Note-Specific)?

- Consistent with "everything is an item" philosophy
- Cross-cutting queries ("show me all urgent items") work across types
- Different users tag different things — the system shouldn't restrict this

## See Also

- [Attachments](item://c0c0c0c0-0005-0000-0000-000000000000) — `attachments` (compositional) vs `parent` (taxonomical)
- [Content Field Conventions](item://c0c0c0c0-0024-0000-0000-000000000000) — Standard field names including `tags`
- [Core Concepts](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000002) — The item model that enables universal tagging
